char.name: Lot
char.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Terah
friends.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.

friends.name: Reuben
friends.bio: Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

posts.author: CHAR_053
posts.body: I lost my wife on the road out. They told us not to look back, and she looked back, toward the home and the life and the daughters married into that city, and she was gone where she stood. I cannot write more about it than that. My daughters and I made it to the hills alive and hollowed out. Far off across the plain I could see the smoke of the whole valley going up like a furnace. I heard later that my uncle Abraham stood at his tent at first light and watched that same smoke rise, and knew. He got me out with his prayers. I could not even get my own wife up a hill.
posts.highlight: Lot's wife looks back and becomes a pillar of salt; Abraham sees the smoke (Gen 19:26-28)

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

posts.author: CHAR_053
posts.body: I went to my sons-in-law in the night and begged them to run. They laughed in my face. They thought I was joking. When dawn broke the messengers were dragging me by the hand, my wife and my two daughters with me, out through the gates because I was still standing there frozen, unable to leave the life I had built in that rotten place. Flee to the hills, they said. Do not stop. Do not look back. We are running now with nothing but each other. I gave up the best land in Canaan for this. I picked the water and did not look at the city. My uncle warned me. Everyone warned me.
posts.highlight: the sons-in-law scoff; the angels pull Lot and his family out at dawn (Gen 19:14-17)

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

posts.author: CHAR_053
posts.body: Two messengers came to the gate at dusk and I brought them under my roof. Before we could rest, every man of Sodom, young and old, from every quarter of the city, surrounded my house and beat on the door. Send the men out to us, they were screaming, so we can rape them. And then I did the thing I will be ashamed of until I die. I went out, shut the door behind me, and offered them my two daughters instead. My own girls. To that. Do to them whatever you want, I said, only leave these men alone. That is what living in Sodom had made of me, and I did not even hear the horror of my own words until they were out. It did not matter. They turned on me, snarling that the foreigner had no right to judge them, and nearly tore the door off to get at me. My guests dragged me back inside and struck the whole mob blind. They still clawed at the wall in the dark, hunting for the door. Then the messengers told me: the LORD has sent us to destroy this place. Get out. I always told myself the stories about this city were exaggerated. God forgive me. I had become one of them.
posts.highlight: the mob demands to rape the visitors; Lot offers his own daughters; the angels blind them (Gen 19:1-11)
posts.ember: how far Sodom had corroded even 'righteous Lot' (2 Pet 2:7-8); a man so at home in a wicked city he would throw his daughters to it

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

posts.author: CHAR_053
posts.body: I chose, and I will not apologize for choosing well. The whole plain of the Jordan is watered like a garden, green as far as the eye can carry, green the way they say Egypt is green. My uncle waved his hand across all of it and told me to take my pick, so I took the best of it. We are moving east, down toward the cities of the plain, toward Sodom. I hear things about Sodom. I am sure they are exaggerated. A man goes where the water and the grass are. That is simply good sense. Isn't it?
posts.highlight: Lot chooses the well-watered Jordan plain and pitches his tent toward Sodom (Gen 13:10-12)

comments.author: CHAR_670
comments.body: Green grass is green grass. But I have kin near those cities, and I would not raise my children in the shadow of Sodom for all the water in the Jordan. Watch yourself down there, friend.

author_info.name: Sela
author_info.bio: My father keeps the well, so I keep the news 💁 everyone passes through eventually. Heard there's strife AGAIN between Abram's herdsmen and Lot's over the watering rights. The land's just not big enough for both, if you ask me.

author_info.name: Lot
author_info.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.
